target.name: Re:Build Manufacturing
target.founder: Miles Arnone, Jeff Wilke, Anthony Manzo, Michael Foley

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $545M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Re:Build Manufacturing is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Miles Arnone, Jeff Wilke, Anthony Manzo, and Michael Foley. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 9 private robotics and manufacturing companies at Series A and Series B stages, plus one public comparable, spanning valuations from $300M to $10.0B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the company's $545M in total raised as a valuation proxy, applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A progression to next-stage rounds, implying a $1.1B valuation. Peer-median analysis across 7 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—including Foundation Robotics ($3.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), and Collaborative Robotics ($500M)—yields a median valuation of $1.6B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $926M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median point of $1.4B. The 2x spread between low and high reflects material uncertainty around growth trajectory and capital efficiency relative to peers. Key sensitivities include relative progress on commercialization milestones, runway, and whether the company trends toward the lower-quartile or upper-quartile of the peer distribution. The peer range itself ($300M–$10.0B) underscores sector-wide heterogeneity in stage and de-risking.
